status_notes: Initial research from AWS Lambda documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live AWS environment.
notes: Serverless compute. Security focus: execution role permissions, VPC configuration, environment variable encryption, code signing, reserved concurrency.

match_nodes.description: IAM role assumed by the function during execution

data.description: Every Lambda function has an execution role — an IAM role that grants the function permission to access AWS services and resources. The function assumes this role when invoked. The role's trust policy allows lambda.amazonaws.com to assume it.
data.vendor_recommendation: One role per function with least-privilege permissions. Use IAM Access Analyzer to generate policies from actual usage. Never reuse roles across unrelated functions.

match_nodes.description: Run Lambda in VPC for private resource access

data.description: Lambda functions can be configured to connect to a VPC, enabling access to private resources (RDS, ElastiCache, internal APIs). When in a VPC, the function loses direct internet access — a NAT gateway is required for outbound internet.

options.name: No VPC (default)
options.description: Function has internet access. Cannot access VPC resources.

options.name: VPC-connected
options.description: Function connects to VPC subnets. Can access private resources. Needs NAT for internet.

default.note: Functions are not VPC-connected by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Only connect to VPC when access to private resources is required. Use multiple subnets across AZs for high availability. Ensure security groups are least-privilege.

match_nodes.description: Encryption of environment variables with KMS

data.description: Lambda environment variables are encrypted at rest using a KMS key. By default, AWS uses a Lambda service key. You can specify a customer managed KMS key for additional control. Environment variables are decrypted when the function execution environment is initialized.

options.name: AWS managed key
options.description: Default Lambda service key. No additional cost.

options.name: Customer managed key
options.description: Your KMS key. Full key policy control, rotation, and audit via CloudTrail.

default.note: Environment variables encrypted with AWS managed key by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use customer managed keys for sensitive environment variables. Consider using AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store instead of environment variables for highly sensitive values.

match_nodes.description: Validate code integrity using AWS Signer

data.description: Code signing for Lambda ensures that only trusted code is deployed. Uses AWS Signer to create signing profiles. When enabled, Lambda checks the code signature on deployment and rejects unsigned or tampered code.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable code signing for production functions to prevent unauthorized code deployment. Configure validation policies to enforce or warn on signature failures.

match_nodes.description: Limit concurrent executions to prevent DoS and runaway costs

data.description: Reserved concurrency sets the maximum number of concurrent instances for a function. Prevents a single function from consuming all available account concurrency. Also prevents runaway invocations and limits blast radius. Setting to 0 throttles all invocations (emergency kill switch).
data.vendor_recommendation: Set reserved concurrency based on expected load and downstream capacity. Use as a throttle to protect downstream services. Monitor throttling metrics.

match_nodes.description: Resource-based policy controlling who can invoke the function

data.description: Resource-based policies (function policies) define which AWS principals and services can invoke the Lambda function. Required for cross-account invocation and service integrations (API Gateway, S3, SNS, etc.). Each statement grants specific actions to specific principals.
data.vendor_recommendation: Review function policies regularly. Avoid granting invoke permissions to overly broad principals. Use conditions (aws:SourceAccount, aws:SourceArn) to restrict service invocations.

match_nodes.description: Public HTTPS endpoint for Lambda function invocation

data.description: Lambda function URLs provide a dedicated HTTPS endpoint for a function. Can be configured with IAM authentication or no authentication (public). Simpler than API Gateway for single-function APIs.

options.name: IAM authentication
options.description: Callers must sign requests with AWS Signature V4. Only IAM principals with lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl can invoke.

options.name: No authentication
options.description: Public endpoint. Anyone can invoke. Use with caution.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use IAM auth or API Gateway instead of public function URLs. If public access is required, implement application-level authentication and rate limiting within the function.

match_nodes.description: Shared code/dependencies — supply chain risk from third-party layers

data.description: Lambda layers package libraries, custom runtimes, or other dependencies for reuse across functions. Layers can be shared across accounts and published publicly. Third-party layers introduce supply chain risk — code runs in your function's execution context with the function's permissions.
data.vendor_recommendation: Audit third-party layers before use. Pin to specific layer versions. Prefer building your own layers from verified sources. Scan layers for vulnerabilities.
